$comment: Lighthouse CI configuration for the Splunk Monitoring Use Cases catalog. Consumed by .github/workflows/lighthouse.yml via the treosh/lighthouse-ci-action — same SHA pin policy as every other third-party action; verified by `python3 -m splunk_uc audit-action-pins`. The config targets the four highest-traffic pages of the static site: the UC explorer (index.html), the scorecard, the clause navigator, and one representative UC permalink (UC-1.1.1) chosen because it is the canonical example throughout AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md. Thresholds are deliberately *warn-only* in this first cut — Lighthouse scores depend on CI runner load and Chrome version drift, so we collect a soak-period baseline before tightening any score floor into a hard gate. The collect.numberOfRuns: 1 setting keeps the workflow under 5 minutes; lift to 3 (the Lighthouse-CI default) once budgets stabilize and we want the trimmed-mean noise reduction.
